info.name: Expert Sourcing Automation Platform
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Expert Sourcing Automation Platform.

## Setup
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Paste a personal platform API key into the `token` collection variable for external API calls
3. Optionally run **Auth > Login (Dev Session)** requests to establish a browser-style session cookie while developing locally
4. For session-based mutating calls, reuse the saved `{{csrfToken}}` value

## Auth Modes
- `{{token}}` is treated as a personal platform API key in Bearer auth headers
- Dev login requests set a session cookie and save `{{csrfToken}}`
- Provider/API-key management endpoints are best exercised with the session flow

## Roles
- **admin** — full access
- **ops** — projects, callers, outreach, screening, operator call tasks
- **caller** — call-tasks/current, call-tasks/:taskId/outcome

item.name: System

request.description: Returns `{"status":"ok"}` if the API process is alive. No auth required.
Pings PostgreSQL and Redis. Returns `{"status":"ready"}` or 500 if a dependency is unreachable.

item.description: Health and readiness checks. No authentication required.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
Creates a local development session cookie and saves the returned CSRF token into `{{csrfToken}}`.

**No auth required. Development/test only.**
Creates a local development session cookie for an ops user and saves `{{csrfToken}}`.
Creates a local development session cookie for a caller user and saves `{{csrfToken}}`.
Returns the authenticated user. Use either a browser session cookie or `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` with a personal platform API key.

Returns `{"status":"admin-ok"}`. Verifies admin-level access.

**Auth:** admin only.

item.description:
Session login and identity verification.

Most collection requests expect `{{token}}` to contain a personal platform API key. Dev login requests are still available for cookie-based local testing.

item.name: API Keys

request.description:
Lists API keys for the signed-in user.

**Auth:** session cookie.

Creates a new personal API key for the current user.

**Auth:** session cookie + `x-csrf-token`.

Revokes a personal API key.

**Auth:** session cookie + `x-csrf-token`.

item.description: Create, list, and revoke personal API keys using a signed-in browser session.

item.name: Projects

body.raw:
{
  "name": "APAC Fintech Experts Q1",
  "description": "Source senior fintech experts in APAC region",
  "targetThreshold": 50,
  "geographyIsoCodes": ["SG", "JP", "AU"],
  "priority": 1,
  "overrideCooldown": false,
  "googleSheetsProviderAccountId": null,
  "supabaseProviderAccountId": null,
  "outreachMessageTemplate": "Hi {{FirstName}}, we'd like to invite you to participate in our project based on your role as {{JobTitle}} at {{CurrentCompany}}. Please let us know if you're interested."
}

request.description:
Creates a new sourcing project. The returned `id` is auto-saved to `{{projectId}}`.

**Required fields:** name, targetThreshold, geographyIsoCodes
**Optional:** description, priority (default 0), overrideCooldown (default false), regionConfig (default {}), googleSheetsProviderAccountId (UUID), supabaseProviderAccountId (UUID), outreachMessageTemplate (string with placeholders: {{FirstName}}, {{LastName}}, {{Country}}, {{JobTitle}}, {{CurrentCompany}})

**Export destinations:** Bind Google Sheets or Supabase provider accounts to automatically export enriched leads. Provider accounts must be created first in Admin → Providers.
Retrieves a project by ID. Returns 404 if not found.
Partial update — send any subset of the create fields (including outreachMessageTemplate).

Attach target companies to a project. Min 1 company.

**Company fields:** name (required), domain, countryIso (2-char ISO), metadata

**Response:** `{"createdOrUpdated": N}`

Add LinkedIn Sales Navigator search URLs to a project. **Minimum 1 search required.**

Each search needs: sourceUrl (URL), normalizedUrl (URL), optional metadata. Metadata can include structured search parameters to improve Apollo sourcing.

**Response:** `{"created": N}`
List active outreach channels bound to a project. Returns array of { channel, label } objects.
List all screening questions for a project.

Create a screening question for a project. The `id` is auto-saved to `{{questionId}}`.

**Fields:** prompt (required), displayOrder (required, int >= 1), required (default true), metadata
Partial update of a screening question.

Permanently delete a screening question and all associated responses (cascade).

**Response:** 204 No Content

item.description:
Project lifecycle management — create projects, attach companies, add Sales Nav searches, manage screening questions.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Callers

request.description:
Register a new caller. The `id` is auto-saved to `{{callerId}}`.

**Required:** email (unique), name, timezone (IANA), languageCodes (min 1), regionIsoCodes (min 1, 2-char ISO)
**Optional:** metadata
Retrieve a caller by ID. Returns 404 if not found.
Partial update — send any subset of the create fields.

Returns the most recent performance snapshot:
- rolling60MinuteDials / Connections / ValidConnections
- shortCallsLastHour
- allocationStatus (ACTIVE, AT_RISK, PAUSED_LOW_DIAL_RATE, etc.)
- performanceScore

item.description:
Caller (phone agent) management — register, update, view performance.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Call Tasks — Caller

request.description: Fetches the caller's currently assigned task, or assigns one if none is active. The `id` is auto-saved to `{{taskId}}`.
Expert expressed interest. A signup chase retry will be created after 24 hours if they don't complete signup.
Expert declined but may be retried later.
Expert permanently suppressed — will never be contacted again.

item.description:
Endpoints for phone agents (callers) to receive and complete call tasks.

**Auth:** caller role. Uses `{{callerToken}}`.

item.name: Call Tasks — Operator

request.description: List all call tasks. Use query params to filter.
List pending tasks waiting to be assigned.
List currently assigned tasks — useful for finding stalled tasks.
List tasks filtered by project.
Requeue a task back to PENDING so it can be reassigned. Optional `reason` field (max 500 chars).

item.description:
Operator endpoints for managing call tasks (list, filter, requeue).

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Job Title Discovery

request.description:
Enqueues an async job that fetches titles from Apollo and uses OpenAI to expand/score them.

**Response:** `{"accepted": true}` (202)

Requires the worker process to be running for processing.

item.description:
Trigger async job title discovery using Apollo + OpenAI.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Outreach

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "expertId": "{{expertId}}",
  "channel": "EMAIL",
  "recipient": "expert@example.com",
  "body": "Hi, we would like to invite you to participate in our research project.",
  "overrideCooldown": false
}

request.description:
Send an email outreach message.

**Response:** `{"accepted": true, "jobId": "..."}` (202)

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "expertId": "{{expertId}}",
  "channel": "LINKEDIN",
  "recipient": "https://linkedin.com/in/expert-profile",
  "body": "Hi, I came across your profile and would love to connect regarding a research opportunity."
}

request.description: Send a LinkedIn outreach message.

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "expertId": "{{expertId}}",
  "channel": "WHATSAPP",
  "recipient": "+14155551234",
  "body": "Hello! We have an exciting opportunity we'd like to discuss with you."
}

request.description: Send a WhatsApp outreach message via 2Chat.

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "expertId": "{{expertId}}",
  "channel": "EMAIL",
  "recipient": "expert@example.com",
  "body": "Following up on our previous conversation — just wanted to check in.",
  "overrideCooldown": true
}

request.description: Send a message bypassing the 30-day cooldown. Use sparingly.

item.description:
Send messages to experts across 13 channels.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

**Channels:** PHONE, EMAIL, LINKEDIN, WHATSAPP, RESPONDIO, SMS, IMESSAGE, LINE, WECHAT, VIBER, TELEGRAM, KAKAOTALK, VOICEMAIL

**Cooldown:** 30-day cooldown between outreach to the same expert. Use `overrideCooldown: true` to bypass.

item.name: Screening

request.description:
Sends the project's screening questions to the expert via their preferred channel.

**Response:** `{"sent": true/false}`

Record an expert's answer to a screening question.

**Response:** `{"accepted": true}`
List screening responses with related question and expert data. Supports filtering by projectId and comma-separated status values. Returns up to 300 results.
Update a screening response's status and/or response text.
Send a follow-up reminder to the expert for pending screening questions in the same project.
Sets the response status to ESCALATED and creates a PENDING call task for a caller to follow up by phone.

item.description:
Dispatch screening questions to experts, record responses, and manage screening workflows.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Providers

request.description:
Creates a Supabase provider account for exporting enriched leads.

**Required credentials:** projectUrl, serviceRoleKey, tableName
**Optional credentials:** schema (default: public)
**Column mappings (optional):** columnEmail, columnPhone, columnCountry, columnCurrentCompany, columnLinkedinUrl, columnJobTitle — specify the exact column names in your Supabase table. If omitted, defaults are used (primary_email, primary_phone, country_iso, company_name, linkedin_url, job_title).

Runs a provider-specific health check. For Supabase, verifies table access and returns row count.

**Response:** `{"healthy": true}` or `{"healthy": false, "error": "..."}`

item.description: Manage provider accounts (create, test, update). Includes Supabase with configurable column mappings for email, phone, country, current company, LinkedIn URL, and job title.

item.name: Documentation Generator

request.description:
Enqueues a documentation generation job. Output is written to `docs/generated/`.

**Response:** `{"accepted": true, "jobId": "..."}` (202)

item.description:
Generate architecture docs, runbooks, state machines, and env checklists.

**Auth:** admin or ops.

item.name: Webhooks

request.description:
Receives Yay.com call events.

**Verification:** HMAC-SHA256 signature via `x-yay-signature` header (signed with `YAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`). Events older than 5 minutes (based on `x-yay-timestamp`) are rejected. Duplicate `x-yay-event-id` values are deduplicated.

**Event types:** call.started, call.ringing, call.answered, call.ended, call.failed, call.recording_ready

**Note:** In production, Yay sends these automatically. This request template is for testing/simulation.

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "sourceUrl": "https://linkedin.com/sales/search/1",
  "normalizedUrl": "https://linkedin.com/sales/search/1",
  "metadata": {},
  "leads": [
    {
      "firstName": "Jane",
      "lastName": "Doe",
      "fullName": "Jane Doe",
      "companyName": "Stripe",
      "jobTitle": "VP of Engineering",
      "linkedinUrl": "https://linkedin.com/in/janedoe",
      "countryIso": "US",
      "emails": ["jane.doe@stripe.com"],
      "phones": ["+14155551234"]
    },
    {
      "firstName": "John",
      "lastName": "Smith",
      "fullName": "John Smith",
      "companyName": "Wise",
      "jobTitle": "Director of Product",
      "linkedinUrl": "https://linkedin.com/in/johnsmith",
      "countryIso": "GB",
      "emails": ["john.smith@wise.com"],
      "phones": []
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Receives LinkedIn Sales Navigator lead data.

**Verification:** Include either `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (LinkedIn OAuth client credentials token) OR `x-sales-nav-client-id` matching stored provider credentials.

**Payload:** projectId (UUID), sourceUrl, normalizedUrl, optional metadata, and an array of leads. Each lead is enqueued individually for ingestion and enrichment.

**Response:** `{"accepted": true}` (202)

item.description: Webhook endpoints for external integrations. These do NOT use JWT auth — they have their own verification mechanisms.

request.description:
Returns the full OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification as JSON. No auth required.

You can also import this URL directly into Postman via **Import > Link**.

item.name: Ranking

request.description: Returns the latest expert ranking snapshots across all projects. Includes expert contacts, project completion summaries, and boost metadata. Rankings are computed every 60 seconds.

query.description: Filter ranking snapshots to a specific project

request.description: Returns expert ranking snapshots filtered to a specific project. Use the projectId variable or paste a UUID directly.

item.description: Expert priority ranking for call allocation. Experts are scored based on project completion deficit and boost tiers (fresh reply, signup chase, callback chase).

item.name: Observability

request.description: Returns 24-hour counts for DLQ items, system events, fraud flags, and processed webhooks.

query.description: SYSTEM, JOB, WEBHOOK, ENFORCEMENT, FRAUD, or ALLOCATION

request.description: Paginated list of all system events. Enable query params to filter by category, entity type, message text, or date range.
Paginated list of dead-letter queue entries. Enable query params to filter by queue name, error text, or date range.
Paginated list of processed webhook events. Enable query params to filter by status, event ID, or date range.
Fraud-flagged call logs and FRAUD/ENFORCEMENT system events. Supports date range filtering and pagination.

item.description: System observability endpoints — unified activity feed, dead-letter queue, webhook log, and fraud/enforcement monitoring.
